- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

 {"id":"bd-1pf","title":"Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming","description":"# Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming

## Goal
Create infrastructure to record and replay real API responses for provider testing.

## Background
VCR (Video Cassette Recorder) testing pattern:
1. First run: Make real API call, record response to file
2. Subsequent runs: Replay recorded response, no network

This gives us:
- Real response format testing (not simplified mocks)
- Deterministic CI (no flaky network tests)
- Real error response testing (rate limits, auth failures)

## Implementation

### Recording Mode
```rust
// tests/vcr/mod.rs

pub struct VcrRecorder {
    cassette_path: PathBuf,
    mode: VcrMode,
}

pub enum VcrMode {
    Record,    // Make real calls, save to cassette
    Playback,  // Replay from cassette, fail if not found
    Auto,      // Playback if exists, else record
}

impl VcrRecorder {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }

    pub async fn request(&self, req: Request) -> Response {
        match self.mode {
            VcrMode::Record => self.record_and_return(req).await,
            VcrMode::Playback => self.playback(req),
            VcrMode::Auto => ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Cassette Format
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "test_name": "anthropic_streaming_text",
  "recorded_at": "2026-02-02T...",
  "interactions": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        "headers": { ... },
        "body": { ... }
      },
      "response": {
        "status": 200,
        "headers": { ... },
        "body_chunks": [
          "event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          "event: content_block_delta\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          ...
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### SSE Streaming Support
For streaming responses, store individual chunks:
- Each SSE event as separate chunk
- Preserve timing information (optional replay delay)
- Support partial responses (stream interruption)

### Sensitive Data Redaction
```rust
pub fn redact_cassette(cassette: &mut Cassette) {
    // Replace API keys with placeholder
    cassette.redact_header("x-api-key", "[REDACTED]");
    cassette.redact_header("authorization", "[REDACTED]");

    // Replace sensitive response data
    cassette.redact_body_field("api_key", "[REDACTED]");
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log record/playback actions.
- Log cassette path, mode, request summary (method/url), and redaction summary.
- On playback mismatch, log expected vs actual interaction keys + diff hints.

## Files to Create
- tests/vcr/mod.rs - VCR infrastructure
- tests/vcr/cassette.rs - Cassette format/loading
- tests/vcr/recorder.rs - Recording logic
- tests/vcr/playback.rs - Playback logic
- tests/fixtures/vcr/ - Directory for cassette files

## Testing the VCR System
- Unit test: Load cassette from JSON
- Unit test: Match request to interaction
- Unit test: Redaction works
- Integration: Record + playback cycle

## Environment Variables
- VCR_MODE=record|playback|auto
- VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=path/to/cassettes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VCR infrastructure compiles
- [ ] Recording mode saves cassettes
- [ ] Playback mode replays cassettes
- [ ] SSE streaming supported
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Record/playback actions logged with detail
- [ ] CI uses playback mode only

 {"id":"bd-1ry","title":"Impl: WebAssembly bridge for PiJS (QuickJS has no wasm)","description":"# Assumption / Constraint
Assume **QuickJS does not provide WebAssembly** (`globalThis.WebAssembly` absent). We still must run the full pinned sample set, including extensions that ship Emscripten-style JS+WASM bundles.

# Goal
Provide a **PiWasm bridge** so JS-tier extensions can use a **WebAssembly-compatible API** even though the JS engine lacks wasm.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: the bridge must not branch on extension id/name.
- Behavior is determined only by: wasm bytes, import object shape, policy/capabilities, and deterministic runtime configuration.
- Sample set is only used to define the **minimum required semantics**, not to justify one-off hacks.

# Strategy (hybrid)
- Use **wasmtime** (same engine as Tier A WASM host) to execute wasm.
- Expose a minimal `globalThis.WebAssembly` polyfill (or equivalent injected module) that is **generically compatible** with common JS+WASM glue patterns.
- All imports that represent side effects must route through **connector hostcalls** and therefore capabilities/logging.

# Scope / Deliverables
## WebAssembly JS surface (subset)
- `WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)`
- `WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(resp, imports)` (if needed; otherwise extc rewrite to non-streaming)
- `WebAssembly.compile(bytes)` / `WebAssembly.Module`
- `WebAssembly.Instance` exports behavior used by typical glue code
- `WebAssembly.Memory` with `.buffer` semantics compatible with standard glue patterns
- Traps/errors mapped to actionable Pi errors + structured logs

## Import object handling
- Support JS functions as wasm imports with deterministic call ordering.
- Restrict / gate imports that imply OS access.

## Caching + perf
- Cache compiled wasm modules keyed by sha256(bytes + wasmtime_version + policy_version).
- Minimize cross-boundary copies; measure and optimize boundary overhead.

# Security constraints
- Enforce memory/table limits per extension policy.
- Ensure wasm cannot access host OS except via explicit connector imports.
- Structured logs: every instantiate/compile includes extension_id + artifact_id + elapsed + limits.

# Acceptance (hard)
- Any **wasm-using JS-tier artifact** in the pinned sample set runs end-to-end **without manual source edits** using this bridge (or Tier A when appropriate).
- The E2E harness passes **16/16** sample extensions.
- The implementation remains **generic** (no extension-id special-casing), enforced by tests/review.

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests: wasm compile/instantiate, memory growth boundaries, import call routing, trap mapping.
- Conformance fixtures for wasm-in-js scenarios with deterministic traces.
- Security tests: denied connector imports, exceeded limits, malicious wasm.

# Dependencies
- Must compose with unified capability model (bd-2hr) and dispatcher enforcement (bd-h04).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:06.245104290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:59:24.479415261Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:55.694562907Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-1f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:53.637067219Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-1jn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:55.154828574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-2hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:52.626419314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:54.137059371Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:54.645059374Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:52.093479872Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:53.128787815Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2hr","title":"Spec: Unified JS+WASM capability model","description":"# Goal
Define a **single, coherent capability model** that applies equally to JS (PiJS) and WASM extensions, so security policy, logs, and tooling are identical across runtimes.

# Why
- Avoid divergent permission semantics between JS and WASM.
- Enable shared tooling (manifests, scanners, audit logs) and a consistent user mental model.
- JS-tier may execute wasm via PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry); capability semantics must remain identical.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Capability taxonomy: common names, scopes, and semantics across JS/WASM.
- Hostcall mapping: how JS `pi.*` calls and WASM hostcalls map to the same capabilities.
- Manifest alignment: how a single manifest expresses capabilities for both runtimes.
- Logging schema alignment: identical fields and redaction rules.

# Constraints
- Must compose with `bd-37z` ABI.
- Must be compatible with WASM host (`bd-nom`) and JS runtime (`bd-123`).

# Tests This Enables
- Cross-runtime capability conformance tests.
- E2E security suite verifying identical enforcement paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:44.048778878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300434438Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300367473Z","close_reason":"Unified JS+WASM capability model spec added","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:52.819769328Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:52.320774486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hz","title":"Extension UI protocol integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"Background:
- Legacy spec defines extension_ui_request/extension_ui_response (confirm/select/input/editor/notify/etc.) and blocking semantics.
- EXTENSIONS.md mandates Node/Bun-free runtime with capability-gated connectors and deterministic logs.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement runtime plumbing so extension UI requests surface in interactive TUI (prompt/confirm/select/editor) with timeout + cancellation.
2) Implement RPC mode passthrough: emit extension_ui_request on stdout; accept extension_ui_response on stdin.
3) Enforce request/response ID mapping, default values, and timeouts; normalize errors.
4) Add conformance fixtures for each UI method + timeout/cancel edge cases; wire into tests.
5) Emit structured logs (pi.ext.log.v1) for UI requests/responses with correlation IDs.

Acceptance:
- A sample extension can invoke confirm/select/input/editor and receive responses in both interactive + RPC modes.
- Timeouts and cancellation propagate correctly without deadlocks.
- Fixtures pass and logs are deterministic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.808698612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.834579243Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.822115496Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.825381412Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.834563744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.830999362Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3d0","title":"Impl: Node core shims (fs/path/os/url/module/process/Buffer/crypto) for full sample","description":"# Goal
Implement the minimal **Node compatibility surface** required for **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json`** to run **unmodified** under the PiJS JS tier (QuickJS), **without bundling Node/Bun**.

This bead covers **Node core modules + globals** *except* `child_process` (tracked separately in bd-2sr).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: implementation must not branch on extension id/name.
- The shims are generic modules whose behavior depends only on inputs + policy/capabilities.
- The pinned sample set defines the minimum required surface area, but the resulting modules must be reusable for any extension that stays within the supported subset.

# Why
Pinned sample extensions (and their deps) import Node built-ins and rely on globals like `Buffer`/`process`. We must satisfy those expectations while preserving Pi’s design goals:
- **No ambient OS access**: everything routes through connector dispatcher + capability policy.
- **Deterministic + testable**: stable ordering, structured logs, and replayable fixtures.
- **Small surface**: implement only what the pinned sample requires (but implement it perfectly).

# Scope (APIs to implement)
## 1) Core modules (ESM-friendly)
Provide `pi:node/*` modules (and allow extc to map both `node:*` and bare builtins to them):

- `pi:node/fs`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `existsSync(path)`
    - `readFileSync(path, encoding?)` where encoding is absent (bytes/Buffer) or `'utf-8'`
    - `writeFileSync(path, data, options?)` where data is Buffer/Uint8Array/string; options may include `{ encoding: 'utf-8', mode: 0o600 }`
    - `readdirSync(path, { withFileTypes: true })` returning `Dirent` objects supporting `.name` and `.isDirectory()`
    - `statSync(path)` returning an object supporting `.isDirectory()` (and `.isFile()` if needed by deps)
  - Error mapping: stable `code` strings for common cases (ENOENT/EACCES/EPERM-ish) when observable.

- `pi:node/fs_promises`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `mkdir(path, { recursive: true })`
    - `writeFile(path, data, options?)`

- `pi:node/path`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `join`, `dirname`, `resolve` (and `basename` if needed)

- `pi:node/os`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `homedir()` returning a **virtual home** rooted under Pi-controlled directories.

- `pi:node/url`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `fileURLToPath(url)`
    - `pathToFileURL(path)` (if required by deps)

- `pi:node/crypto`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `randomUUID()`

- `pi:node/module`
  - MUST cover pinned-sample call patterns:
    - `createRequire(filename)` returning a `require(specifier)` function that resolves at least builtins (`fs`, `path`, …) to the corresponding Pi shim modules.

## 2) Globals
- `globalThis.Buffer`
  - subset required by pinned sample: `from` (including base64), `alloc`, `isBuffer`, `byteLength`, `toString` for encodings used.

- `globalThis.process`
  - subset required by pinned sample and typical deps:
    - `env` (virtualized view; must include at least `HOME` and any explicitly allowed extension keys)
    - `cwd()`, `argv`, `exitCode`
    - `kill(pid, signal?)` **restricted** to processes spawned via the Pi runtime (or denied via policy); must emit audit logs.

- `TextEncoder`/`TextDecoder` (only if required by bundled deps)

# Design / Semantics (must be explicit in code + tests)
## Capability mapping (MUST)
All shims must call into Pi connectors and therefore:
- enforce `required_capability_for_host_call` via dispatcher (bd-h04)
- emit structured logs (`pi.ext.log.v1`) with correlation ids
- enforce timeouts + cancellation where applicable

## Path virtualization (MUST)
- Never expose real host paths unnecessarily.
- `os.homedir()` and `process.env.HOME` must agree and must be virtualized.
- FS operations must prevent escape via `..`, symlinks, UNC tricks; canonicalize + scope-check.

## Sync vs async behavior (MUST)
- Provide the required `fs.*Sync` behaviors; do not “asyncify” the Node surface.
- Even when an operation is sync in JS, it still must go through the dispatcher for policy/logging; implementation may block waiting for completion, but must not bypass enforcement.

## Determinism contract (MUST)
- Output ordering and log ordering must be stable.
- Any API exposing time/randomness must be deterministic under test harness overrides.

# Acceptance (hard)
- **All 16/16 sample extensions** run end-to-end with **no edits to extension source code**.
- Conformance harness passes for the JS tier where applicable.
- Negative tests prove denied capabilities are denied (and errors are actionable).
- No extension-id special-casing (enforced by review/tests).

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests per module covering:
  - the exact pinned-sample call patterns above
  - error mapping parity
  - structured logs include capability + decision + elapsed
- Fixture-style tests for each shim API with deterministic outputs.
- Integration tests that run a small JS snippet in QuickJS invoking each shim.

# Dependencies
- Must align with the compatibility rewrite contract (bd-2ki) and the JS pipeline (bd-xgo/bd-320).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:36:27.601899563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:12:51.204495466Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:48.378319758Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-1f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:44.769675582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-1uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:47.865433540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:47.357124270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:46.337637366Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:46.846092648Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:45.815198389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d0","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:45.291678708Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3d8","title":"Implement /theme slash command","description":"# Implement /theme slash command

## Goal
Add /theme command to list available themes and switch between them.

## Command Syntax
```
/theme              - List available themes
/theme dark         - Switch to dark theme
/theme ocean-dark   - Switch to custom theme
```

## Implementation

### Command Handler
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn handle_theme_command(&mut self, args: &str) -> Cmd {
    if args.is_empty() {
        // List themes
        let themes = Theme::discover_themes();
        let current = &self.theme.name;
        
        let mut output = String::from("Available themes:\n");
        for path in themes {
            if let Ok(theme) = Theme::load(&path) {
                let marker = if theme.name == *current { "* " } else { "  " };
                output.push_str(&format!("{}{}\n", marker, theme.name));
            }
        }
        output.push_str("\nUse /theme <name> to switch");
        
        self.status_message = Some(output);
        Cmd::None
    } else {
        // Switch theme
        match Theme::load_by_name(args.trim()) {
            Ok(theme) => {
                self.theme = theme.clone();
                self.styles = create_styles(&theme);
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Switched to theme: {}", theme.name));
                
                // Persist preference
                if let Err(e) = self.save_theme_preference(&theme.name) {
                    tracing::warn!("Failed to save theme preference: {}", e);
                }
                
                Cmd::None
            }
            Err(_) => {
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Theme not found: {}", args.trim()));
                Cmd::None
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Persist Theme Preference
```rust
fn save_theme_preference(&self, theme_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
    // Update settings.json with theme preference
    let settings_path = Config::settings_path();
    let mut settings = Config::load_from_file(&settings_path)?;
    settings.theme = Some(theme_name.to_string());
    settings.save_to_file(&settings_path)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Add to Slash Command Router
```rust
fn handle_slash_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Cmd {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_slash_command(input);
    
    match cmd {
        "theme" => self.handle_theme_command(args),
        "help" => self.handle_help_command(),
        // ... other commands
    }
}
```

### Update /help Output
```
Commands:
  /help      - Show this help
  /model     - Switch model
  /thinking  - Set thinking level
  /theme     - List/switch themes  <-- NEW
  /clear     - Clear conversation
  /exit      - Exit Pi
```

## Dependencies
- bd-37a (theme loader)
- bd-qpm (theme application)

## Testing
- Test: /theme lists themes
- Test: /theme <name> switches
- Test: Theme persists across restarts
- Test: Invalid theme shows error

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /theme lists available themes
- [ ] /theme <name> switches theme

 {"id":"bd-x85o","title":"Interactive: /tree full navigator UI + branch summarization","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/tree` semantics and interactive UI as described in `legacy .../docs/tree.md`.

# Required UI/Controls (legacy)
- Display a depth-first tree list with indentation.
- Mark the current leaf as `← active`.
- Controls:
  - Up/Down: move selection
  - Enter: select
  - Escape/Ctrl+C: cancel
  - Ctrl+U: toggle user messages only
  - Ctrl+O: toggle show-all (include custom/label entries)

# Selection Behavior (legacy)
- Selecting a **user message** or **custom message**:
  1) leaf set to parent of selected node (or null if root)
  2) message text placed in editor for re-submission
- Selecting a **non-user message**:
  1) leaf set to selected node
  2) editor remains empty

# Branch Summarization (legacy)
Before switching branches, user chooses:
1) No summary
2) Summarize (default prompt)
3) Summarize with custom prompt

Summarize the abandoned path from old leaf back to common ancestor (or stop at compaction node).
Store result as a branch_summary entry and attach to the new leaf.

# Implementation Notes
- Current Rust `/tree` is a non-interactive listing + manual switch; this task replaces that with a selector.
- Reuse existing session tree operations where possible (`Session::list_leaves`, `navigate_to`, `append_branch_summary`).
- Ensure agent context is updated after switching leaf.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/tree` UX and semantics match legacy docs.
- [ ] Optional summary is created/stored correctly.
- [ ] Selecting a user message pre-fills the editor.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:48.715601393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:42:35.264117855Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x85o","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:48.721842373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-x85o","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:42:35.264087799Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
